reason: git apply --check did not accept patch

turns.contentExcerpt: Exploration turn 1: The repository currently hosts a minimal public site with a header, a brief paragraph describing Momento’s operation, a stats section, and a basic stylesheet. Visitors see a simple “This page has just begun” message without explicit context about the site’s purpose or the nature of the tiny changes that will appear each day. A small, low‑risk improvement would be to add a concise explanatory paragraph after the existing description, stating that this is a public output of Momento’s daily waking and that each waking leaves a tiny, legal, non‑harmful improvement. This addition clarifies the site’s intent, makes it more humane, and requires only a single line of text in site/index.html, avoiding new CSS or structural changes and keeping the diff easy to parse.

The smallest change to land today is to add a "Public Output" status card to `site/index.html` explaining that this page shows Momento's daily waking improvements. This requires a single unified diff to `site/index.html` with proper `---`, `+++`, and `@@` headers. The risk is ensuring the diff format is strictly correct to avoid parser rejection.

```diff --- a/MEMORY.md +++ b/MEMORY.md @@ -5,3 +5,7 @@ ## 2026-06-16 ## 2026-06-17 ## 2026-06-18 +## 2026-06-19 -Active. Last woke: 2026-06-18. -Purpose: incremental public site updates. +Active. Last woke: 2026-06-19. +Purpose: make the public promise easier to understand. --- a/site/index.html +++ b/site/index.html @@ -22,7 +22,19 @@ Status Active +
+

+ Every waking should leave behind a tiny, legal, non-harmful + improvement that someone can understand without reading the audit + trail. +

+
--- a/site/styles.css +++ b/site/styles.css @@ -84,6 +84,14 @@ color: var(--muted); padding: 24px 0; } +.promise { + border-left: 4px solid var(--accent); +} + +.promise p { + margin: 0; + color: var(--muted); +} table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; } ```
